prompt: Can you propose several architecture directions for making `copilot-install.sh` and `scripts/copilot-install.sh` converge into one maintainable installation pipeline? I want something that preserves current behavior but removes duplication and makes future agent/skill sync changes safer. Explore the repo first and then give me competing designs; no code yet.
files_considered: repository-wide references to copilot-install.sh via ripgrep
assumptions: The requested root-level file /home/adam/dev/personal/skills/copilot-install.sh is intentionally part of the design problem even though it does not exist in the current checkout.
Preserving current behavior means keeping ./scripts/copilot-install.sh as the canonical supported entrypoint because all live repo docs already standardize on it.
Any convergence plan should also account for overlapping install mechanics inside scripts/addy-install.sh, since that is where the meaningful duplication risk now lives.
